name: mac-optimizer
description: Diagnose macOS storage, cache, logs, startup items, developer-tool residue, and routine maintenance needs with a diagnosis-first workflow, dry-run previews, risk-ranked recommendations, safe cleanup scripts, rollback guidance, and high-risk manual review boundaries.
author: Chuluu
outputs: Low, medium, and high risk recommendations
use_cases: Diagnose whether a Mac still has useful optimization space
Preview routine cache and log cleanup safely
Summarize low, medium, and high risk maintenance options
Run monthly macOS maintenance with conservative defaults
Review high-risk storage cleanup before taking manual action
not_for: Bypassing Time Machine, Docker volume, simulator data, or system service review
Deleting user-created documents, photos, source code, or project directories
Replacing backup strategy or device-management tooling

scripts.name: diagnose
scripts.description: Generates the read-only macOS diagnostic report and latest recommendation data.

scripts.name: quick
scripts.description: Runs routine low-risk maintenance after diagnostic data exists.

scripts.name: one_click
scripts.description: Runs the standard maintenance flow with dry-run, safe mode, and confirmation support.

scripts.name: verify
scripts.description: Checks that the local Mac Optimizer toolset is complete and runnable.

scripts.name: rollback
scripts.description: Restores tool-managed macOS preference changes where applicable.

scripts.name: monthly
scripts.description: Orchestrates monthly diagnostics and optional maintenance.

scripts.name: install
scripts.description: Installs the skill into common local agent skills directories.

scripts.name: package_runtime_skill
scripts.description: Creates a lightweight runtime ZIP for team and partner installation in compatible agent tools.

safety.high_risk_policy: High-risk actions are report-only and manual-review only unless a future workflow adds separate, explicit, narrow, reversible confirmation steps.

compatibility.notes: Core workflow uses SKILL.md, references, scripts, and local macOS shell tools. agents/openai.yaml is Codex-specific UI metadata and can be ignored by other runtimes. Keep future runtimes in expected until verified end to end.

related_skills.name: business-website-skill
related_skills.description: Create client-ready business websites with evidence mapping, visual-system discovery, implementation, and QA.
